---
title: "End-of-course self reflection (SR #3)"
author: "Jay Kim"
date: 2026-03-13
format: pdf
---

::: callout-note
*For the full assignment description and grading specifications, please see the course website: <https://eds-240-data-viz.github.io/course-materials/assignments/SR3-endcourse.html>*
:::

## Reflect on your progress

-   [x] **1. Since your mid-quarter self-reflection, have you improved any of your existing skills? Have you learned new ones?**

    -   Since my mid-quarter reflection, I’ve continued to improve several of the skills I mentioned earlier, especially my use of color in visualizations and my selection of data visualization tools. Earlier in the quarter, I mostly relied on what looked good, but now I feel like I understand much better why certain color choices (such as restricting color hue to seven hues) work better than others and how they affect readability and accessibility. On top of that, the chart type for the visualization I want to convey to my audience is much clearer with the resources provided by the class. I also feel more confident thinking about how different audiences might interpret a visualization. In addition, I’ve improved my coding workflow when building visualizations, and I feel more comfortable experimenting, troubleshooting, and adjusting things when something doesn’t look right the first time. I am still working on adding less detail (e.g., using different hues vs. a single color for the bar plot), and eventually I will feel comfortable keeping my title left-aligned rather than center-aligned.

-   [x] **2. Have you accomplished your learning goals for this quarter (that you set in SR #1, and potentially expanded upon or modified in SR #2)? If you did, how did you do it?**

    - For the most part, yes. My main goal for the quarter was to become more comfortable designing thoughtful, effective visualizations, and I feel like the assignments and in-class exercises helped me make significant progress toward that goal. Having regular opportunities to practice and experiment with different design choices made a big difference. I also found myself paying more attention to visualizations outside of class, which turned out to be a fun way to reinforce what we were learning, as I started thinking more critically about what worked well and why.

-   [x] **3. What are you proudest of accomplishing in this course?**

    - I think I’m most proud of how much more confident I feel when designing visualizations. At the beginning of the course, I often felt unsure about which design decisions were best, but now I feel much more comfortable making those choices and explaining the reasoning behind my choices of visual elements. Prior to this course, I chose based on my intuition, which may not have worked very well, as I had no foundation or knowledge of why I chose the type of chart I am using. I’m also proud of how much I’ve learned about color and typography in visualization, since that was the topic that excited me the most earlier in the quarter. 

-   [x] **4. What are some transferable skills that you developed in this course? How might you apply them to other courses or deliverables within your graduate program (e.g. MEDS, MESM, etc.) or to jobs in the future?**

    - One of the biggest transferable skills I developed in this course is learning how to communicate data clearly through visualization. Being able to present complex information accurately, accessibly, and easily is useful in almost any field, especially in data science and environmental work. I also developed an intuition for how to assess whether a visualization is biased. Are you trying to convey the truth or trying to mislead the audience? These skills will be helpful in the future when I need to present analysis results or create reports, and I will also use them when communicating insights to audiences without a technical background.

## (Optional) Share any feedback you might have about the class

-   [x] **1. What is one thing that you really liked about this course, and why? What is one thing you think could be improved about this course, and how?**

    - I think the class was really engaging, and all the TED talks were great too! I wish this course could be split into 240A and 240B so I could take two quarters' worth of data visualization, because there is just so much more I can learn! Not that you need any improvement in class, per se, but I just wanted to show how much I appreciated this class.

-   [x] **2. Is there anything else you'd like the instructors to know about your experience in the course?**

    - Thank you for teaching this class, Sam and Anne! You guys have given me tremendous help, and I really enjoyed the class. I wouldn't change a single thing about the course, and I hope you both will teach this class again (or future seminars / workshops). I would like to join this class again after graduation to improve my skills! :') I will really miss this class!
